texto: ...she wasn't reading Deathly Hallows at all. Her book wasn't orange but rose and water and sand, and featured a kid on a broomstick and white unicorn. Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone. She didn't notice me staring at her. 'Oh, I envy you,' I thought, but was smiling for her. She had just begun.
explicacao: Citação de Melissa Anelli sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “...she wasn't reading Deathly Hallows at all. Her book wasn't orange but rose and water and sand,…” articula reflexao, deathly-hallows, harry-potter em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e harry-potter.

frase_original: ...she wasn't reading Deathly Hallows at all. Her book wasn't orange but rose and water and sand, and featured a kid on a broomstick and white unicorn. Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone. She didn't notice me staring at her. 'Oh, I envy you,' I thought, but was smiling for her. She had just begun.
